column1: # GIGANTIC upload_manifest.tsv — one_direction_homologs / BLOCK_diamond_ncbi_nr
# Publishes the one_direction_homologs deliverables: the cross-species hit
# statistics summary, the per-species hit statistics, and the per-species
# top-hits tables. Source globs are relative to this manifest's directory
# (the workflow-RUN_*-species70_burst/ root).
# Build with (from the one_direction_homologs subproject root):
#   bash RUN-update_upload_to_server.sh
# (which calls ../../server/ai/update_upload_to_server.py --subproject-dir .)
# Published files land at:
#   ../../upload_to_server/BLOCK_diamond_ncbi_nr/workflow-RUN_*-species70_burst/N-output/
# The publisher creates SYMLINKS (follow-the-symlink at HTTP request time, §38),
# so the large per-species top-hits tables cost no extra disk to publish.
# NOTE: dir_label is a per-DIRECTORY folder subtitle. The per-species statistics
# and top-hits tables both live in 5-output/, so both entries share one dir_label.
# This is a COPYME template (single source of truth). The discovery helper
# ignores manifests in workflow-COPYME-* dirs; copy this file into the
# canonical workflow-RUN_* dir for it to publish (per gigantic_conventions §38/§39).
source_path
# ----- Headline summary: cross-species hit statistics (6-output) -----
OUTPUT_pipeline/6-output/6_ai-all_species_statistics.tsv
column4: NCBI nr hit statistics — all species
column6: One row per species: total query proteins processed and the counts of self-hits, non-self-hits, queries with no non-self hit, and queries with no self hit. A self-hit is a top-10 NCBI nr DIAMOND hit with 100 percent identity, zero mismatches and gaps, and at least 95 percent query coverage (the query protein is present in NCBI nr); a non-self-hit is a true homolog with a different sequence. Master summary across all 70 species.

column1: # ----- Per-species hit statistics (one file per species; filename is the phyloname) -----
OUTPUT_pipeline/5-output/*_statistics.tsv
column6: The per-species form of the hit statistics: total queries and self / non-self hit counts for one species. The filename is the species phyloname.
column8: per-species hit statistics and top hits

column1: # ----- Per-species top NCBI nr hits (one file per species; filename is the phyloname) -----
OUTPUT_pipeline/5-output/*_top_hits.tsv
column6: One row per query protein: its top 10 NCBI nr DIAMOND hits (hit IDs, NCBI descriptions, and e-values), plus the top non-self hit (ID, description, percent identity) and the top self hit (ID, description). Large per-protein table (up to roughly 47 MB / 34,000 rows per species). The filename is the species phyloname.
column8: per-species hit statistics and top hits
